coverage_notes: Good coverage across recreational and master runners, with one large cross-sectional study (n=3124), multiple marathon prediction studies, a narrative review of predictive models, a review of anthropometric predictors in endurance/ultra-endurance, and an IOC consensus on REDs covering the health risks of low energy availability. Populations are predominantly recreational male runners; limited coverage of elite female runners specifically. No running-specific meta-analysis on body composition and performance was found; the closest is the Gallant 2025 systematic review/meta-analysis on REDs prevalence across sports. The Fogelholm 1994 review is older but foundational for weight reduction effects on sports performance.

papers.title: Running performance in a timed city run and body composition: A cross-sectional study in more than 3000 runners
papers.notes: Cross-sectional study of 3124 runners examining whether body composition (via BIA) predicts running speed better than BMI in a timed city run.

papers.title: Marathon performance in relation to body fat percentage and training indices in recreational male runners
papers.journal: Open Access Journal of Sports Medicine
papers.notes: Developed a predictive equation (r=0.81) for marathon time using body fat percentage, weekly training distance, and mean training pace in 126 recreational male marathoners.

papers.title: Running speed during training and percent body fat predict race time in recreational male marathoners
papers.journal: Open Access Journal of Sports Medicine
papers.notes: Multivariate regression analysis identifying training speed and percent body fat as the two strongest predictors of marathon race time in recreational male runners.

papers.title: Does Muscle Mass Affect Running Times in Male Long-distance Master Runners?
papers.notes: Investigated whether skeletal muscle mass or body fat was associated with race times in 349 male master runners across half-marathon, marathon, and ultra-marathon distances.

papers.title: Relationship Between Body Segment Mass and Running Performance in Well-Trained Endurance Runners
papers.notes: Examined correlations between DXA-measured leg, arm, and trunk segment masses and 5000m personal best times in 37 well-trained endurance runners.

papers.title: Anthropometric profiles and body composition of male runners at different distances
papers.notes: Compared anthropometric profiles and body composition of 68 male academic athletes across sprint, middle-distance, and long-distance running events.

papers.title: Predictive Performance Models in Long-Distance Runners: A Narrative Review
papers.journal: International Journal of Environmental Research and Public Health
papers.notes: Narrative review of 58 studies examining predictive models for 5K through marathon performance, including anthropometric variables and body composition as predictors.

papers.title: Relationship of anthropometric and training characteristics with race performance in endurance and ultra-endurance athletes
papers.notes: Narrative review examining how anthropometric and training characteristics predict race performance across swimming, cycling, running, and triathlon in endurance and ultra-endurance events.
